item.title: Microsoft Threatens Security Researcher | Palo Alto VPN Exploited | Google Insider Trading Case
item.description: Microsoft's dispute with a former security researcher takes a dramatic turn as the company raises the possibility of criminal action over the publication of proof-of-concept code for unpatched zero-day vulnerabilities. David Shipley examines the escalating conflict between Micros…

item.title: Hackers are exploiting Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN authentication bypass (CVE-2026-0257)
item.description: Authentication bypass vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-0257) in Palo Alto Networks’ firewalls that the company disclosed on May 13 have been targeted in “limited exploit attempts”. “Across multiple customers, Rapid7 observed successful exploitation via authentica…

item.title: Press Release: CSO30 ASEAN & Hong Kong Awards 2026 open for nominations
item.description: >The CSO30 ASEAN & Hong Kong Awards return in 2026, as an important moment to recognise the cybersecurity leaders and teams who are making resilience measurable across the region. In a landscape shaped by rapid threat evolution, board-level scrutiny and rising expectations of…

item.title: Governing shadow AI without killing innovation
item.description: In this Help Net Security video, Alan Snyder, CEO at NowSecure, talks about governing shadow AI without stopping innovation. He frames the problem as two opposing forces. Companies need to adopt AI fast because attackers and competitors will outpace them otherwise, but they also …

item.title: 145 AI laws passed in 2025 and privacy teams aren’t catching a break
item.description: 145 AI-related laws were enacted by state legislatures in 2025, and more than 1,000 additional bills were introduced or revised, according to DataGrail’s Privacy and AI Trends Report 2026. Average cost of manual data subject request management (Source: DataGrail) Shadow AI …

item.title: OWASP Agent Memory Guard: Stop AI agents from being weaponized through their own memory
item.description: AI agents keep memory across sessions. Conversation history, vector stores, scratchpads, and RAG indexes persist between runs, and anything written into that store becomes a privileged input the agent reads back later. An attacker who plants text in the wrong field can override a…

item.title: 6 critical security gaps every CISO must address
item.description: CISOs acknowledge that no organization is completely safe, but many also admit their security measures aren’t where they’d like them to be. One-third of CISOs surveyed for Proofpoint’s 2025 Voice of the CISO Report said the data within their organization is not adequately protect…

item.title: Asimily turns device risk into automated network policy
item.description: Asimily has launched Segmentation Orchestration, enabling connected-device risk intelligence to flow directly into enforceable network policy without manual translation. No other platform combines full asset visibility, vulnerability prioritization, and segmentation orchestration…

item.title: Palo Alto Warns High-Severity Bug Is Being Actively Exploited
item.description: A vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS software is being exploited in attacks

item.title: NVIDIA goes open source with a big batch of physical AI agent tools
item.description: NVIDIA just dropped a big batch of open-source “physical AI” skills and tools, and they’re designed to make a roboticist’s life a whole lot easier. The idea? Take the messy, complicated work behind robots, self-driving cars, vision AI, and industrial digit…

item.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
item.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites. WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed custom…

item.title: Helping defense's use of AI catch up with offense, cost of the vulnpocalypse, news - ESW #461
item.description: Interview with Evan Powell - Generative and agentic AI are improving cyberattacks faster than they're improving cyber defenses. Offensive folks have been having the most luck with AI so far, which is further eroding any advantage defenders might have had. Evan Powell joins us to …

item.title: Top 4 data security best practices for the AI-enabled enterprise
item.description: To maximize AI’s value without increasing security risk, organizations must enforce best‑practice data protections across their environment.

item.title: Dashlane hit by brute-force campaign triggering account suspensions
item.description: Dashlane has confirmed that a brute-force attack over the weekend triggered a wave of account suspension emails, unusual login notifications, and authentication issues. The password manager says the incident was caused by attacks against individual accounts rather than a breach o…

item.title: EU organizations buckle under rising compliance pressure
item.description: Cybersecurity governance in the EU is shifting under expanding frameworks such as NIS2 and DORA, while AI raises new questions for security teams. What the future brings is hard to predict, and organizations must find a way to cope. Antonija Vojnović, Governance, Risk and Complia…

item.title: Data discovery gaps that catch enterprises off guard
item.description: In this interview with Help Net Security, Avani Desai, CEO at Schellman, talks about the gap between what organizations think they know about their data and what discovery scans turn up. She shares stories of shadow data in abandoned cloud storage, post-merger surprises where dup…

item.title: 1,000 Data Breaches Later, the Disclosure Lag is Worse Than Ever
item.description: Presently sponsored by: Report URI: Guarding you from rogue JavaScript! Don’t get pwned; get real-time alerts & prevent breaches #SecureYourSite Today, I loaded the 1,000th data breach into Have I Been Pwned . Reflecting on that milestone number, I pondered how to mark the occasi…

item.title: Weekly Update 506
item.description: Presently sponsored by: Report URI: Guarding you from rogue JavaScript! Don’t get pwned; get real-time alerts & prevent breaches #SecureYourSite I'm finding it quite fascinating to watch the current spate of ShinyHunters breaches and dumps. There's the obvious criminali…

item.title: Edmunds - 177,860 breached accounts
item.description: In January 2026, the automotive research and car-shopping platform Edmunds was listed by the ShinyHunters hacking group as having been breached . Data purportedly obtained in the incident was later published publicly and included 178k unique email addresses, usernames, passwords,…

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 1st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9952, (Mon, Jun 1st)
item.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

item.title: DNS-AID lets AI agents find and verify each other through DNS
item.description: AI agents run across many platforms, and each one needs a way to locate and confirm the identity of the others it works with. The Linux Foundation’s DNS-AID project gives them that capability through the Domain Name System, the same address lookup system that has directed i…

item.title: Election threats are focused on campaign systems, not voting machines
item.description: Check Point said actors are shifting toward campaign systems and AI-generated content, outpacing the public's ability to understand and respond to the risks. The post Election threats are focused on campaign systems, not voting machines appeared first on CyberScoop .

item.title: Attackers Abuse Shared Content for ChatGPT Phishing Campaign
item.description: Push Security says threat actors are delivering malware hosted on chatgpt.com/s/ domain

item.title: A week in security (May 25 – May 31)
item.description: A list of topics we covered in the week of May 25 to May 31 of 2026

item.title: Rapid7 and Exclusive Networks Expand Partnership Across the Nordics
item.description: Building stronger cybersecurity outcomes together The cybersecurity landscape across the Nordics is evolving rapidly. Organizations are facing increasing pressure to modernize security operations, reduce complexity, and respond faster to threats, all while navigating growing regu…

item.title: Containers on fire: from container escapes to supply chain attacks
item.description: We break down the primary attack vectors in containerized environments: exposed secrets, privilege misconfigurations, API compromise, and supply chain attacks.

item.title: Payment apps are watching what you say (Lock and Code S07E11)
item.description: This week on the Lock and Code podcast, we speak with Rainey Reitman about financial censorship that boots customers off major payment apps.

item.title: Unidentified RAT pushes NetSupport RAT, (Mon, Jun 1st)
CrowdStrike Scales AI-Native Agents Across Falcon Exposure Management with NVIDIA
CrowdStrike Brings Enterprise-Grade Security to the AI Factory with NVIDIA Vera BlueField-4 STX
Infosecurity Europe: OWASP Forms New Agentic Research Council
item.description: OWASP’s new Agentic Research Council will aim to connect academic work to operational realities on agentic AI security

item.title: Your phone called. It needs a cleanup.
item.description: Introducing Android Junk Cleaner. It scans your phone for leftover files, temporary data, and outdated caches that build up and slow down your device.

item.title: How Canva scaled to 260+M users while elevating security and productivity
item.description: See how Canva uses 1Password to integrate new teams fast, empower developers and maintain high standards for customers.

item.title: Websites Can Now Spy on You Through Your Hard Drive
item.description: Thanks to the newly detailed FROST technique, telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

item.title: CVE-2025-15504 lief-project LIEF ELF Binary Parser.tcc parse_binary null pointer dereference

CVE-2024-36137 A vulnerability has been identified in Node.js, affecting users of the experimental permission model when the --allow-fs-write flag is used.
Node.js Permission Model do not operate on file descriptors, however, operations such as fs.fchown or fs.fchmod can use a "read-only" file descriptor to change the owner and permissions of a file.

CVE-2024-22018 A vulnerability has been identified in Node.js, affecting users of the experimental permission model when the --allow-fs-read flag is used. This flaw arises from an inadequate permission model that fails to restrict file stats through the fs.lstat API. As a result, malicious actors can retrieve stats from files that they do not have explicit read access to. This vulnerability affects all users using the experimental permission model in Node.js 20 and Node.js 21. Please note that at the time this CVE was issued, the permission model is an experimental feature of Node.js.

CVE-2025-23167 A flaw in Node.js 20's HTTP parser allows improper termination of HTTP/1 headers using `
X` instead of the required `

`. This inconsistency enables request smuggling, allowing attackers to bypass proxy-based access controls and submit unauthorized requests.
The issue was resolved by upgrading `llhttp` to version 9, which enforces correct header termination.
Impact: * This vulnerability affects only Node.js 20.x users prior to the `llhttp` v9 upgrade.
CVE-2026-46242 eventpoll: fix ep_remove struct eventpoll / struct file UAF
CVE-2026-42790 nameConstraints DNS bypass via subject CommonName fallback in public_key hostname verification
CVE-2026-42012 Gnutls: gnutls: certificate validation bypass due to improper handling of uri and srv sans
CVE-2026-9804 Kubevirt: kubevirt: vmexport directory symlink escape enables exporter pod file read
CVE-2026-48864 Libsolv: heap buffer overflow in libsolv repopagestore via unchecked decompression of malicious .solv page data
CVE-2026-48962 IO::Compress versions before 2.220 for Perl can execute arbitrary code in File::GlobMapper via an attacker-controlled output glob
CVE-2026-40034 gitoxide - Command Injection via Partial .gitmodules Override in gix-submodule
CVE-2026-40528 OpenSC < 0.27.0 Buffer Overrun in do_key_value() via profile.c
CVE-2026-40510 OpenSC < 0.27.0-rc1 Stack Buffer Overflow via piv_process_history() in card-piv.c
CVE-2026-42789 Non-CA certificate accepted as intermediate issuer in public_key path validation
CVE-2026-42013 Gnutls: gnutls: certificate validation bypass due to oversized subject alternative name
CVE-2026-42015 Gnutls: gnutls: memory corruption due to off-by-one error in pkcs#12 bag handling
CVE-2026-5260 Gnutls: gnutls: information disclosure via heap overread in rsa key exchange
CVE-2026-7374 Kubevirt: kubevirt virt-handler: privilege escalation and node compromise via symlink following vulnerability
CVE-2025-15649 IO::Uncompress::Unzip versions before 2.215 for Perl propagate uncaught exception when parsing zip header with malformed DOS date
CVE-2026-44839 RabbitMQ: Unsanitized vhost names allow for XSS in management UI

item.title: WP Maps Pro bug exploited to create admin accounts on WordPress sites
item.description: Hackers are targeting WordPress websites running a vulnerable version of the WP Maps Pro plugin, which allows creating rogue administrator accounts without authentication. [...]

item.description: In this special edition of CyberWire Daily’s 10th anniversary series, N2K CyberWire's Maria Varmazis and Dave Bittner consider the tactics, trends, and turning points that shaped the threat landscape over the last decade of ransomware. Ransomware has evolved from small-scale exto…

item.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
item.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks. The bot network, per the Dutch Politie and the National Cyber Security Center (NCSC), …

item.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
item.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

item.title: GPS: A backbone for critical infrastructure.
item.description: Since its original creation in the 1970s, GPS has evolved from a technology primarily used by the military to a foundation for modern society. After the removal of selective availability for civilians in 2000, GPS’s value has significantly expanded. In the past two decades, nearl…

item.title: The evolution of GPS.
item.description: This week on T-Minus: Space-Cyber Briefing: we look at GPS and how this technology has become instrumental to modern society. As governments have expanded the public’s use of this technology it has evolved from a fringe service to one that supports many of the modern day services…

item.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that c…

item.title: CVE-2026-41184 ServiceAccount token disclosure via install-cni container logs
Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]

item.title: Law enforcement and industry disrupt criminal infrastructure.
item.description: Researchers blame Iranian government for LA transit authority hack. Extortion group sends individuals to infiltrate organizations in person.

item.title: New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
item.description: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'CIFSwitch' in the Linux kernel could allow attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions, abuse the kernel's key request mechanism, and gain root privileges. [...]

item.title: Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
item.description: The one-click vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on self-hosted Flowise servers by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow. The post Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: AI Sees Trees, Humans See Forests
item.description: AI systems are becoming extremely effective at processing security logs, compliance data, and operational telemetry at massive scale. In cybersecurity environments, that creates major efficiency gains for analysis, monitoring, and identifying patterns humans might miss manually. …

item.title: Russia-aligned crime group Greyvibe extensively uses AI in attacks
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a previously undocumented Russian group that makes extensive use of large language models (LLMs) in its attacks against private, government, and military organizations in Ukraine. It uses a variety of attack vectors along with custom malware, with the g…

item.title: Cybercrime Crew Claims It Hacked Mike Lindell’s MyPillow
item.description: Plus: A ransomware group is now stealing data in person, BusPatrol wants to hand its license plate surveillance data to the cops, and more.

item.title: Atlas Menu - 63,926 breached accounts
item.description: In May 2026, the GTA V and CS2 cheat service Atlas Menu suffered a data breach. An attacker claimed to have gained access to all Atlas systems and published the service's database to a public GitHub repository. The incident exposed 64k unique email addresses along with usernames,…

item.title: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
item.description: A dependency confusion campaign leveraged 33 malicious npm packages to collect reconnaissance data from developer and build environments. This report details the attack chain, observed tradecraft, and detection opportunities to help organizations identify and disrupt related acti…

item.title: The skills pay the bills.
item.description: Today we are joined by Marco Giuliani, Vice President & Head of Research at ThreatDown, discussing their work on "GachiLoader adopts AI skill lure." Threat actors are now using fake AI agent “skills” as highly convincing social engineering lures, with a new campaign disguising th…

item.title: Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
item.description: Moscow’s agents are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen and deploying cyber spies and hackers who gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure. The post Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say appe…

item.title: IBM and Red Hat want to become the ‘security clearinghouse’ for open source applications in the enterprise
item.description: Open source code is everywhere in the enterprise; it’s estimated that upwards of 90% of Fortune 500 companies have it in their software supply chains. But open source code is notoriously rife with vulnerabilities, and identifying and patching those bugs can be an endless battle f…

item.title: CVE-2026-46219 spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after-free on unbind
CVE-2026-46214 vsock/virtio: fix accept queue count leak on transport mismatch
CVE-2026-46137 mptcp: pm: ADD_ADDR rtx: fix potential data-race
CVE-2026-46186 Bluetooth: virtio_bt: validate rx pkt_type header length
CVE-2026-46172 ipv6: xfrm6: release dst on error in xfrm6_rcv_encap()
CVE-2026-46168 mptcp: fix scheduling with atomic in timestamp sockopt
CVE-2026-46163 wifi: b43legacy: enforce bounds check on firmware key index in RX path
CVE-2026-46131 KVM: x86: check for nEPT/nNPT in slow flush hypercalls
CVE-2026-46110 net: stmmac: Prevent NULL deref when RX memory exhausted
CVE-2026-46128 ipmi: Check event message buffer response for bad data
CVE-2026-42250 Off-by-One Leading to Out-of-Bounds Write in bzip2
CVE-2026-46191 fbcon: Avoid OOB font access if console rotation fails
CVE-2026-46159 btrfs: fix btrfs_ioctl_space_info() slot_count TOCTOU which can lead to info-leak
CVE-2026-46226 spi: fsl: fix controller deregistration
CVE-2026-46165 openvswitch: vport: fix self-deadlock on release of tunnel ports
CVE-2026-46158 mptcp: pm: ADD_ADDR rtx: always decrease sk refcount
CVE-2026-46232 HID: playstation: Clamp num_touch_reports
CVE-2026-46197 drm/amdkfd: validate SVM ioctl nattr against buffer size
CVE-2026-46220 drm/amdgpu/sdma4: replace BUG_ON with WARN_ON in fence emission
CVE-2026-46107 dm-thin: fix metadata refcount underflow
CVE-2026-46176 RDMA/mlx5: Fix error path fall-through in mlx5_ib_dev_res_srq_init()
CVE-2026-46149 scsi: target: configfs: Bound snprintf() return in tg_pt_gp_members_show()
CVE-2026-46208 batman-adv: stop tp_meter sessions during mesh teardown
CVE-2026-46116 xfrm: defensively unhash xfrm_state lists in __xfrm_state_delete
CVE-2026-46225 spi: rspi: fix controller deregistration
CVE-2026-46236 media: rc: xbox_remote: heed DMA restrictions
CVE-2026-46164 btrfs: fix double free in create_space_info_sub_group() error path
CVE-2026-46235 media: saa7164: add ioremap return checks and cleanups
CVE-2026-46127 RDMA/ocrdma: Don't NULL deref uctx on errors in ocrdma_copy_pd_uresp()
CVE-2026-46177 ipmi: Add limits to event and receive message requests
CVE-2026-46155 smb/client: fix out-of-bounds read in smb2_compound_op()
CVE-2026-46157 ALSA: pcm: oss: Fix data race at accessing runtime.oss.trigger
CVE-2026-46136 wifi: mt76: mt7921: fix a potential clc buffer length underflow
CVE-2026-46132 net: rtnetlink: zero ifla_vf_broadcast to avoid stack infoleak in rtnl_fill_vfinfo
CVE-2026-46170 mptcp: pm: ADD_ADDR rtx: free sk if last
CVE-2026-46190 mtd: spi-nor: debugfs: fix out-of-bounds read in spi_nor_params_show()
CVE-2026-46230 drm/amdgpu/vcn3: Prevent OOB reads when parsing dec msg
CVE-2026-46175 f2fs: fix fsck inconsistency caused by FGGC of node block
CVE-2026-46123 Bluetooth: virtio_bt: clamp rx length before skb_put
CVE-2026-46238 batman-adv: stop caching unowned originator pointers in BAT IV
CVE-2026-46120 ip6_gre: Use cached t->net in ip6erspan_changelink().
CVE-2026-46108 ipmi:si: Return state to normal if message allocation fails
CVE-2026-46152 wifi: mac80211: drop stray 'static' from fast-RX rx_result
CVE-2026-46112 RDMA/hns: Fix unlocked call to hns_roce_qp_remove()
CVE-2026-46114 RDMA/rxe: Reject non-8-byte ATOMIC_WRITE payloads
CVE-2026-46122 wifi: b43: enforce bounds check on firmware key index in b43_rx()
CVE-2026-46146 ALSA: usb-audio: Avoid potential endless loop in convert_chmap_v3()
CVE-2026-46125 wifi: mac80211: remove station if connection prep fails
CVE-2026-46227 sctp: revalidate list cursor after sctp_sendmsg_to_asoc() in SCTP_SENDALL
CVE-2026-46153 8021q: delete cleared egress QoS mappings
CVE-2026-46150 fanotify: fix false positive on permission events
CVE-2026-46241 spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after-free on registration failure
CVE-2026-46147 KVM: arm64: Fix pin leak and publication ordering in __pkvm_init_vcpu()
CVE-2026-46135 nvmet-tcp: fix race between ICReq handling and queue teardown
CVE-2026-42496 Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract symlinks with attacker controlled targets outside the extraction directory
CVE-2026-46189 RDMA/vmw_pvrdma: Fix double free on pvrdma_alloc_ucontext() error path
CVE-2026-9538 Archive::Tar versions before 3.10 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via attacker controlled entry size field in tar header
CVE-2026-46199 drm/amdgpu/vcn4: Prevent OOB reads when parsing dec msg
CVE-2026-46151 usb: usblp: fix heap leak in IEEE 1284 device ID via short response
CVE-2026-42497 Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract hardlinks to attacker controlled paths outside the extraction directory

CVE-2026-46181 RDMA/mlx4: Fix mis-use of RCU in mlx4_srq_event()

CVE-2026-46231 batman-adv: bla: put backbone reference on failed claim hash insert
CVE-2026-46200 spi: mpc52xx: fix controller deregistration
CVE-2026-46209 drm/gem: Fix inconsistent plane dimension calculation in drm_gem_fb_init_with_funcs()

CVE-2026-46198 batman-adv: fix integer overflow on buff_pos
CVE-2026-46111 Bluetooth: hci_conn: fix potential UAF in create_big_sync
CVE-2026-46195 smb: client: validate dacloffset before building DACL pointers
CVE-2026-46194 f2fs: fix node_cnt race between extent node destroy and writeback
CVE-2026-46109 usb: ulpi: fix memory leak on ulpi_register() error paths
CVE-2026-46229 drm/amdkfd: Clear VRAM on allocation to prevent stale data exposure
CVE-2026-46173 exit: prevent preemption of oopsing TASK_DEAD task
CVE-2026-46160 btrfs: fix missing last_unlink_trans update when removing a directory
CVE-2026-46180 wifi: brcmfmac: Fix potential use-after-free issue when stopping watchdog task
CVE-2026-46115 block: add pgmap check to biovec_phys_mergeable
CVE-2026-46185 smb/client: fix out-of-bounds read in symlink_data()
CVE-2026-46161 md/raid10: fix divide-by-zero in setup_geo() with zero far_copies
CVE-2026-46212 batman-adv: bla: prevent use-after-free when deleting claims
CVE-2026-46205 staging: media: atomisp: Disallow all private IOCTLs
CVE-2026-46234 vsock: fix buffer size clamping order
CVE-2026-46171 riscv: kvm: fix vector context allocation leak
CVE-2026-46179 ASoC: SOF: Don't allow pointer operations on unconfigured streams
CVE-2026-46196 tracepoint: balance regfunc() on func_add() failure in tracepoint_add_func()

CVE-2026-46145 RDMA/mana: Validate rx_hash_key_len
CVE-2026-46133 RDMA/rxe: Reject unknown opcodes before ICRC processing
CVE-2026-46129 btrfs: fix double free in create_space_info() error path
CVE-2026-46218 drm/amdgpu: Add bounds checking to ib_{get,set}_value
CVE-2026-46204 drm/amdgpu/vcn4: Prevent OOB reads when parsing IB

Notepad++ vulnerabilities could enable arbitrary code execution on Windows systems
item.description: Two arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities in Notepad++ let local attackers run commands of their choice on Windows machines by tampering with the editor’s XML configuration files, with both flaws rated High at CVSS 7.8. The flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-48778 and CVE-2026-48800, …

item.title: New infostealer reaches enterprise devices through FortiClient EMS vulnerability
item.description: Attackers are delivering a broad-spectrum infostealer to enterprise computers by exploiting a known vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS). “The [malicious] payload was presented as a Fortinet endpoint update and executed through Fo…

item.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible Marimo network using a recently disclosed vulnerability. "The attacker compromi…

item.title: Federal audit reveals NIST’s NVD is plagued by poor planning and duplication
item.description: A report from the Commerce Inspector General details how mismanagement allowed a backlog of 27,000 unprocessed security flaws to grow unchecked, while the agency duplicated work with a similar CISA program. The post Federal audit reveals NIST’s NVD is plagued by poor planni…

item.title: Rapid7 Observed Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (CVE-2026-0257)
item.description: Overview On May 13, 2026, Palo Alto Networks published a security advisory for CVE-2026-0257, a medium severity authentication bypass affecting PAN-OS and Prisma Access when a specific configuration is present. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability allows a remote unauthe…

item.title: Metasploit Wrap Up 05/29/2026
item.description: More Linux LPEs Hark the age of the Linux LPE has arrived. This week’s release follows up on recent work bringing new Linux LPEs to Metasploit users. Copy Fail seemed to have kicked off a trend of similar bugs and hot on its heels is Dirty Frag. Dirty Frag is actually two vulnera…

item.title: Microsoft and security researcher’s dueling posts about cybersecurity disclosures get nasty
item.description: Microsoft and a prominent cybersecurity researcher have gotten into a very public and rather personal exchange of unpleasantries about what responsible cybersecurity disclosures should mean in 2026. A cybersecurity researcher going by the name Nightmare Eclipse, who has disclosed…

item.title: Lack of response to critical vulnerability in Gogs is a reminder of the limits of open source projects
item.description: A newly discovered and so far unpatched critical vulnerability in the open source Gogs Git service not only demands immediate action from developers to secure their code, it also puts a spotlight on the potential issues in using self-hosted code platforms from small maintainers. …

item.title: Building a risk-based vulnerability management program that scales
item.description: In this Help Net Security video, Shankar Somasundaram, CEO at Asimily, explains how to build a risk-based vulnerability program. He notes that vulnerabilities are exploding by an order of magnitude in the age of AI-driven attacks, with one customer finding a thousand vulnerabilit…

item.title: GDPR set the tone for regulatory action — and the AI fine pushback to come
item.description: Big tech firms continue to push back against fines levied for alleged violations of European data protection law, in what could be a harbinger for AI regulations to come. While lawyers and experts quizzed by CSO broadly argue that big tech firms contesting data protection rules i…

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Opus 4.8, prepares Mythos-class models for all customers
item.description: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.8 and outlined plans for broader access to its Mythos-class models, which the company expects to make available to all customers in the coming weeks. Claude Opus 4.8 (Source: Anthropic) Claude Opus 4.8 is available to all users, with pricing u…

item.title: The Gentlemen are coming for your files, and then your network
item.description: Ransomware operators have spent years refining the art of locking files. Now, some are working harder to get those lockers to every reachable system first. Microsoft’s recent warning of the Gentlemen ransomware revealed its operators using a self-propagating Go-based encryptor ca…

item.title: Chinese Hackers Exploit Iran War to Target Maritime and Energy Companies
item.description: ESET’s 2026 APT Activity Report suggests China-backed APTs are using instability in the region to target victims, as well as continuing activity against organizations around the globe

item.title: Cybersecurity trends in SEC filings
item.description: In 2023, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) required public companies to include a new section in their 10-K annual filings that is devoted to cybersecurity. This section is meant to address “cybersecurity risk management, strategy, governance and incidents.” I got curi…

item.title: Chrome security update addresses 22 critical severity flaws
item.description: Google has released a major Chrome security update that fixes 151 vulnerabilities in the browser, including 22 critical-severity flaws. While no actively exploited zero-days were disclosed, the unusually large number of vulnerabilities and the predominance of internally discovere…

item.title: Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities
item.description: The browser update resolves critical-severity security defects that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome 148 Update Patches 151 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: LinkedIn-themed phishing abuses Adobe’s A/B testing platform
item.description: A newly documented phishing campaign is targeting professionals with fake LinkedIn business emails and abusing a trusted service operated by Adobe. The attack from the victim’s perspective The attack starts with an email that looks, at first glance, like a routine business …
